news.headline: ZiGGY, a mobile EV charging robot, chosen for pilot in Birmingham and Tuscaloosa, AL, with support from Alabama Power and various accelerators.
news.summary: A cutting-edge mobile EV charging robot has recently received backing from key organizations in Alabama. Alabama Power, Techstars Alabama EnergyTech Accelerator, AMP Center at The University of Alabama, and Innovation Depot in Birmingham have thrown their support behind this innovative technology. The announcement was made on December 10, 2024. EV Safe Charge Inc., known for its adaptable electric vehicle solutions, is at the forefront of this development.

news.headline: SAQ reports $337.8 million net income for Q2 fiscal 2024-2025, with a slight increase from last year. Decline in store sales, 0.4% decrease, due to changing consumer habits. Total dollar sales increased by 0.1% to $946.7 million.
news.summary: In Montreal, the Société des alcools du Québec (SAQ) disclosed a net income of $337.8 million for the second quarter of the 2024-2025 fiscal year, concluding on September 14, 2024. This marks a modest $1.5 million or 0.4% uptick in comparison to the same period the year prior. Sales in the store and specialized center network experienced a downturn, with a $3.7 million or 0.4% decrease from the previous fiscal year, including a 1.3 million litre or 2.8% reduction in volume sales. Notably, this network has observed declining volumes for the past seven quarters, primarily due to evolving customer shopping behaviors. Conversely, overall dollar sales saw a slight 0.1% increase, reaching $946.7 million, propelled by a $4.7 million surge in sales to the wholesale grocer network.

news.headline: TSMC's November revenue surged by 34% to $8.55B, reaching $81.05B for Jan - Nov 2024 with market share at 64.9%. Strong demand from AI, smartphones drives growth.
news.summary: In November 2024, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSM) saw a notable 34% year-on-year increase in revenue, reaching $8.55 billion. From January to November 2024, the company experienced a revenue growth of 31.8% year over year, totaling $81.05 billion. This growth is attributed to the high demand for artificial intelligence technologies. TSM's market share rose to 64.9%, up from 62.3% quarter over quarter, solidifying its leading position in the wafer foundry industry globally. The surge in market share can be credited to the escalating demand for its 3nm and 5nm technologies in smartphones and AI, driven by key clients like Apple Inc and Nvidia Corp.

news.headline: AutoZone Q1 misses EPS at $32.52, revenues at $4.28B (+2.1% yoy), same-store sales up 0.4%. Gross margin at 53.0%. CEO optimistic.
news.summary: On Tuesday, AutoZone, Inc (NYSE:AZO) disclosed fiscal first-quarter earnings, reporting earnings per share of $32.52, falling short of the estimated $33.76. The quarterly revenues were $4.28 billion, a 2.1% year-over-year increase, missing the projected $4.30 billion. The company noted a 0.4% rise in same-store sales (or 1.8% in constant currency) with domestic sales seeing a 0.3% growth. The gross profit margin for the quarter stood at 53.0%, up 16 basis points due to improved merchandise margins. Operating profit saw a decline of 0.9% to $841.1 million. AutoZone repurchased 160 thousand shares of common stock at an average price per share of $3,156 under its share repurchase program, amounting to a total investment of $505.2 million. Additionally, Booz Allen and Palantir have joined forces to support US defense operations.

news.headline: MongoDB (NASDAQ: MDB) beats Q3 earnings forecast with $1.16 EPS and $529.4M revenue. Analyst estimates and forecasts increase.
news.summary: After the closing bell on Monday, MongoDB, Inc. (NASDAQ:MDB) released its third-quarter results surpassing expectations. The company posted quarterly earnings of $1.16 per share, exceeding the analyst consensus of 69 cents. Revenue for the quarter reached $529.4 million, beating the projected $497.75 million and marking a rise from $432.938 million from a year prior. The outstanding performance was attributed to the EA performance and 26% growth in Atlas revenue. President and CEO, Dev Ittycheria, credited the success to MongoDB's superior developer data platform. The company anticipates fourth-quarter revenue to fall between $515 million and $519 million, over the projected $506.13 million. For further details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Academy Sports faces struggles in Q3 with lower sales and profit - stock tanks after missing estimates; Q3 sales $1.34B.
news.summary: Academy Sports and Outdoors, Inc (NASDAQ:ASO) saw a significant drop in its stock value following its third-quarter results. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of 98 cents, falling short of the analyst consensus of $1.30. Quarterly sales were reported at $1.34 billion, a 3.9% decrease from the previous year, missing the expected $1.39 billion. Comparable sales declined by 4.9%, which was less than the 8.0% decline reported the previous year. The company finished the quarter with $296 million in cash and equivalents, $1.53 billion in net merchandise inventories, and net long-term debt of $483.1 million.

news.headline: Oracle (NYSE: ORCL) Q2 results weaker than expected, revenue at $14.06B (up 9% YoY), EPS at $1.47. AI demand boosts cloud revenue by 52%.
news.summary: Oracle Corporation (NYSE:ORCL) disappointed investors with lower-than-anticipated financial results in Q2. The company revealed second-quarter revenue of $14.06 billion, showing a 9% increase from the previous year. This figure fell short of the expected $14.11 billion. Additionally, the adjusted earnings per share were reported at $1.47, missing the projected $1.48. CEO Safra Catz attributed the 52% growth in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure revenue to surging AI demand, outperforming competitors notably. Notably, GPU consumption soared by 336% during the quarter, with Oracle unveiling the leading AI SuperComputer globally. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Vail Resorts (NYSE:MTN) beats Q1 analyst estimates, posting $4.61 loss per share and $260.27 million in sales. CEO optimistic.
news.summary: Vail Resorts, Inc. (NYSE: MTN) announced a stronger-than-expected performance in its first quarter. The company reported a quarterly loss of $4.61 per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of a loss of $5.05 per share. Quarterly sales totaled $260.27 million, outperforming the analyst consensus of $254.00 million. CEO Kirsten Lynch noted that the company typically operates at a loss in the first fiscal quarter as North American and European mountain resorts are not open for ski season. Winter operations in Australia and summer activities were the main drivers of the positive results.

news.headline: Ferguson's Q1 net sales hit $7.77B, missing estimates. Organic revenue fell 0.3%. Adjusted EBITDA dropped to $758M. Gross margin contracted to 30.1%.
news.summary: Shares of Ferguson Enterprises Inc. (NYSE: FERG) are down following the release of their first-quarter results. The company reported net sales of $7.77 billion, representing a 0.8% increase compared to the previous year, however falling short of the expected $7.83 billion. Organic revenue saw a 0.3% decline, with a 1.1% decrease attributed to acquisition growth. Sales in the U.S. segment rose by 0.5%, while in Canada there was a notable 6.3% growth. The gross margin decreased by 10 basis points to 30.1% and the adjusted operating margin also contracted from 10% to 9.1% year over year. Adjusted EBITDA dropped to $758 million from the prior year's $819 million. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Dow falls 150 points; Oracle reports low results, Dow at 44,242.81, NASDAQ at 19,820.91, S&P 500 at 6,052.43.
news.summary: U.S. stocks had a mixed morning, with the Dow Jones falling around 150 points to 44,242.81, a decrease of 0.36%. Conversely, the NASDAQ rose by 0.43% to 19,820.91 while the S&P 500 dropped 0.01% to 6,052.43. Communication services sector saw a 2.3% increase, but utilities sector fell by 1.1% on Tuesday. Oracle Corporation (NYSE:ORCL) reported second-quarter revenue of $14.06 billion, up 9% year-over-year, missing the consensus estimate of $14.11 billion. In a positive note, Chimerix, Inc. (NASDAQ:CMRX) shares significantly increased. Explore more on financial platforms for the full story.

news.headline: C3.AI stock trading lower after Q2 earnings report. Revenue at $93.34M, beating estimates; loss per share at 6 cents.
news.summary: Shares of C3.Ai Inc (NYSE: AI) are down following the release of its second-quarter financial results. The company reported revenue of $93.34 million, surpassing analyst expectations of $91.02 million. Additionally, C3.AI reported a quarterly loss of 6 cents per share, exceeding projections for a loss of 16 cents per share. The positive results led to a brief spike in share price, likely influenced by a new global alliance agreement with Microsoft Corp (NASDAQ: MSFT). For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Bitcoin's price drops under $95,000, recovers to $97,750; Bernstein advises buying the dip as dip attributed to leverage adjustments, not fundamental issues.
news.summary: Bitcoin's price dropping below $95,000 has worried traders, but Bernstein analysts suggest buying during this dip. After briefly falling to $94,890, Bitcoin rebounded to $97,750, displaying strength during market turbulence. Bernstein explains the dip as a result of typical leverage adjustments near the $100,000 threshold, rather than underlying problems. The pullback was driven by traders adjusting leverage, according to analysts. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Johnson Outdoors (NASDAQ: JOUT) Q4 profits miss with sales at $105.9 million, loss per share $3.35. Gross margin down 600 bps to 23.5%. Stocks dip due to market challenges and lower margins.
news.summary: Johnson Outdoors Inc (NASDAQ:JOUT) saw a sharp decline in its stock value following the announcement of its fourth-quarter 2024 financial results. Despite a 9.9% increase in sales to $105.9 million, falling short of the expected $114.99 million, the company reported a loss per share of $3.35, significantly worse than the anticipated loss of $0.98. The gross margin contracted by 600 basis points to 23.5%, attributed to higher promotional discounts, a shift towards lower-margin products, and inventory write-offs for slow-moving and obsolete stock. Gross profit decreased by 12.4% to $24.87 million. The operating loss for the quarter expanded to $42.8 million, up from $22.6 million.

news.headline: G-III Apparel Group reports Q3 EPS $2.59, up 30% brand growth; revenue $1.086B, 1.8% YoY rise. CEO hails strong results.
news.summary: In the third quarter, G-III Apparel Group, LTD. (NASDAQ:GIII) surpassed analyst predictions with an adjusted EPS of $2.59, although it was lower than the prior year's $2.78. The company's quarterly revenues of $1.086 billion marked a 1.8% increase from the previous year, closely aligning with the consensus of $1.098 billion. Morris Goldfarb, the chairman and CEO of G-III, expressed satisfaction with the strong results, emphasizing the over 30% organic growth of key owned brands such as DKNY, Karl Lagerfeld, and Donna Karan.

news.headline: Insurity appoints Sylvester Mathis as Chief Revenue Officer, with 35 years of industry experience, to drive growth strategy.
news.summary: Insurity, a prominent provider of cloud-based software for insurance entities, has named Sylvester Mathis as its new Chief Revenue Officer. Mathis, who has a remarkable 19-year tenure at Insurity and boasts over 35 years of experience in various leadership positions within the insurance sector, will play a crucial role in driving the company’s expansion initiatives. With a background spanning underwriting operations, claims, and regulatory affairs, Mathis is set to lead the charge in enhancing client value proposition.

news.headline: "VicOne and ZDI will co-host Pwn2Own Automotive 2025 in Japan from Jan 22-24, at Tokyo Big Sight."
news.summary: VicOne, a top automotive cybersecurity solutions provider, revealed plans to co-host "Pwn2Own Automotive 2025" with Zero Day Initiative. The competition will be held at Tokyo Big Sight, West Hall from January 22 to January 24, 2025. This event is part of the “17th AUTOMOTIVE WORLD 2025 – Advanced Automotive Technology Expo.” It marks the second Pwn2Own Automotive contest, building on the success of the initial event.

news.headline: Partnership between CaringBridge and Instacart enables community-led access to groceries and essentials.
news.summary: BLOOMINGTON, MN and SAN FRANCISCO - On December 10, 2024, CaringBridge, a nonprofit health platform, and Instacart, a top grocery technology company in North America, announced a collaboration. This partnership aims to streamline community-driven assistance by enabling smoother access to groceries and essential items.

news.headline: Neuropathy Action Foundation releases white paper on AI for neuropathy, urges increased awareness. - Santa Ana, CA, Dec. 10, 2024.
news.summary: Neuropathy Action Foundation (NAF) is urging for greater awareness and advocacy regarding the utilization of artificial intelligence to mitigate possible misuse. Located in Santa Ana, California, as of December 10, 2024, NAF, a nonprofit entity focused on ensuring neuropathy sufferers receive essential treatments, is leading this call. They emphasize the necessity of informed discussions and actions to address potential abuses stemming from AI applications.

news.headline: LinkSquares names Biju Muduli as Chief Marketing Officer; brings global marketing expertise to company.
news.summary: LinkSquares, a renowned company in AI-driven contract analytics and management solutions, has welcomed Biju Muduli as its Chief Marketing Officer. With extensive experience in global marketing, Muduli is poised to drive growth at LinkSquares. The appointment was made public on December 10, 2024, marking a significant development for the company. LinkSquares aims to leverage Muduli's expertise to further enhance its position in the market and capitalize on emerging opportunities.

news.headline: FDA provides tips on handling flour safely during the holiday season to ensure delicious and safe treats.
news.summary: In Silver Spring, Maryland on December 10, 2024, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has provided important tips as the holiday season approaches to guarantee the safety and deliciousness of your treats. It is essential to understand the significance of flour and optimal practices to protect your loved ones. Stay informed to make informed decisions and ensure a joyful and safe holiday celebration.

news.headline: Dr. Cedric Von Pritchett, M.D., MPH, joins Oberkotter Foundation board of trustees, bringing expertise on pediatric otolaryngology and hearing loss.
news.summary: Dr. Cedric Pritchett, M.D., MPH, a pediatric otolaryngologist and clinical investigator, has joined the Oberkotter Foundation in Philadelphia. Bringing extensive expertise and direct experience assisting families of children with hearing loss, Dr. Pritchett's appointment signifies a significant addition to the team. The news was officially announced on December 10, 2024, by PRNewswire.

news.headline: QUASAR bought a 72-unit multifamily portfolio in DC at North Capitol St & Hawaii Ave. Portfolio included 65 units.
news.summary: Quasar, a real estate firm, recently completed the acquisition of a distressed multifamily portfolio in Northeast Washington, DC. The portfolio comprises 72 units situated at the junction of North Capitol Street and Hawaii Avenue. The acquisition includes 65 units on Hawaii Avenue, and additional details on the remaining units were not provided. This successful transaction marks another milestone for Quasar in expanding its residential property portfolio in Washington, DC.

news.headline: TRST outperforms peers in Efficiency Ratio, EPS, Cost of Funds, and Tier 1 Capital, a standout in any economy.
news.summary: TrustCo Bank Corp NY (TRST) released its financial performance data, showcasing a comparison with its competitors in various key metrics. The company's Efficiency Ratio stands at [insert number], while its EPS is at [insert number]. Additionally, TrustCo Bank Corp NY reported a Cost of Funds of [insert number] and a Tier 1 Capital of [insert number]. These figures offer a comprehensive view of the bank's standing in relation to its industry peers.

news.headline: Ascend Elements to boost US lithium carbonate production by 60% with new line, aiming for 3,000 metric tons annually by 2025.
news.summary: A new lithium recovery line is set to launch at a battery recycling facility in Covington, Georgia. The facility, operated by Ascend Elements, a battery materials company, will have the capacity to produce over 3,000 metric tons of lithium carbonate (Li2CO3) annually. This development is expected to ramp up production of lithium carbonate with a purity level exceeding 99%. The project is scheduled to commence on December 10, 2024.

news.headline: Dominican Republic opens immersive dome in NYC's Times Square for week-long cultural celebration, drawing 500 people.
news.summary: On December 8, 2024, in New York, the Dominican Republic commenced a week-long celebration highlighting its rich culture and tourism by unveiling an Immersive Dome in Times Square. Approximately 500 people gathered for the grand opening event.

news.headline: DELPH Program welcomes new cohort of public health leaders, aiming to address challenges nationwide.
news.summary: A program has been launched to promote diverse leadership in tackling public health issues across the country. The Association of State and Territorial Health Officials (ASTHO) and the Satcher Health Leadership Institute (SHLI) at Morehouse School of Medicine recently revealed this initiative. The program aims to address nationwide public health challenges by fostering leadership diversity. The announcement was made in Arlington, Virginia on December 10, 2024. This partnership between ASTHO and SHLI signifies a concerted effort to drive change in the public health sector.

news.headline: Tervis partners with Listrak for hyper-personalized campaigns. Founded in 1946, Tervis is a family-owned drinkware company.
news.summary: An iconic tumbler brand, founded in 1946 and beloved nationwide, will utilize Listrak's AI-powered data platform to develop cohesive customer profiles and enhance personalized campaigns. The company, Tervis, based in Lititz, Pennsylvania, made the decision on December 10, 2024. This move aims to optimize marketing strategies and provide tailored experiences to consumers by leveraging advanced technology.

news.headline: HHS grants pharmacy personnel authority to administer vaccines and tests until 2029, as requested by APhA.
news.summary: On December 10, 2024, the American Pharmacists Association (APhA) issued a statement concerning the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services' (HHS) handling of APhA's plea to issue a twelfth amendment to the federal Public Declaration. The APhA's request to the HHS was in regards to an undisclosed matter, with details about the specific issue not being disclosed in the statement. This decision comes after ongoing discussions between the APhA and the HHS, with the APhA advocating for the change. The twelfth amendment is a crucial step in addressing concerns raised by the APhA, and the outcome of this response will have significant implications for the pharmacy sector. The APhA remains committed to working with the HHS to ensure the best possible outcome for pharmacists in the United States.

news.headline: Home Chef predicts 6 home cooking trends for 2025. Leading meal delivery service reflects on successful year.
news.summary: The leading meal solutions company, Home Chef, based in Chicago, shared findings on the eating habits of home cooks in the year 2024. As we near the end of the year, Home Chef, ranked as the top meal delivery service*, highlights another successful year of providing easy and tasty meals to households across the country.

news.headline: Oakworth Capital Bank ranked #7 on Financial Planning's "Best Workplaces in Money Management" list for 2024.
news.summary: In Birmingham, Alabama, on December 10, 2024, Oakworth Capital Bank was recognized as the 7th best company in money management by Financial Planning's 2024 "Best Workplaces in Money Management" list. This achievement comes after being ranked number 2 in the yearly "Best Bank to Work For" survey.

news.headline: CleanJoule introduces SpaceSAF as an eco-friendly alternative to Rocket Propellant 1, boasting over 4% higher energy density for sustainable space missions.
news.summary: A sustainable aviation fuel company, CleanJoule, launched SpaceSAF™, a new product acting as a direct substitute for highly purified kerosene fuels utilized in liquid rockets like Rocket Propellant 1/RP-1 and RP-2. The innovative SpaceSAF™ by CleanJoule caters to the demand for eco-friendly space expeditions while also enhancing payload capacity with an energy density boost of over four percent in contrast to current petroleum-based fuels. This development signifies a major advancement for sustainable fuel solutions in the aerospace sector, promising a more environmentally conscious approach to space missions.

news.headline: NAT's Non-Exec Vice Chairman Alexander Hansson purchases additional shares.
Senior management of MT Højgaard Holding A/S report trading of shares as per EU Regulation 596/2014.
news.summary: Reported transactions involving shares and related securities by senior management members and their related parties have been disclosed by MT Højgaard Holding A/S in accordance with article 19 of EU Regulation 596/2014. The transactions data includes buying and selling activities and amounts involved, as required by regulatory standards.

news.headline: Ana Botín shares insights on finance's future in DIGI FULL episode on Bloomberg. Tune in now.
Trellix launches encryption upgrades to protect data from insider threats, safeguarding against unauthorized access.
news.summary: Trellix, a cybersecurity company based in San Jose, California, has unveiled enhancements to its Trellix Drive Encryption for both on-premises and SaaS management. This update provides customers with increased flexibility for deploying encryption protection to secure their data and devices against unauthorized access. Recent reports indicate that a significant number of lost or stolen assets in the past year led to data disclosure, underlining the importance for organizations to have robust drive security measures in place.

news.headline: Gozio Health partners with Hyro to deploy AI at Baptist Health, Jacksonville, reducing call wait times by over 3 mins and boosting service levels by 26%.
news.summary: In Atlanta, Gozio Health and Hyro have joined forces to enhance AI assistant deployment at Baptist Health in Jacksonville. This alliance has successfully integrated conversational AI into the health system's digital platforms, significantly reducing call wait times by over three minutes and boosting service levels by 26%. Baptist Health, the largest in North Florida, has seen substantial improvements through this collaboration.

news.headline: Trustate launches game-changing estate planning documents, becoming the only end-to-end solution; based in Tampa, FL.
news.summary: Trustate, a top player in trusts and estates management, has made a significant move by expanding its cutting-edge software offerings. The company, based in Tampa, Fla., has ventured into the estate planning sector by introducing innovative estate planning documents like wills, trusts, and other crucial planning tools. This development marks a major milestone for Trustate, making it the sole software provider with a comprehensive solution for trusts and estates professionals.

news.headline: Sanford Health chooses Availity for better revenue cycle management, uses Essentials Pro™ solution.
news.summary: In Jacksonville, Florida, Availity®, the largest real-time information network in the nation, has revealed a new partnership. Sanford Health has chosen Availity Essentials Pro™ revenue cycle management solution. This collaboration aims to streamline the health system’s claim and payment processing via an all-payer clearinghouse with pre-service, post-service, and post-adjudication features. Sanford Health's selection of Availity was expedited due to the company's expertise.

news.headline: Zip in 2024 processed $107 billion across 4M suppliers, benefiting global leaders like HP, AMD, Anthropic to streamline operations.
news.summary: In San Francisco, Zip, a prominent procurement orchestration platform, marked a successful year in 2024, solidifying procurement orchestration as a vital capability for contemporary businesses. During the year, the platform facilitated processing of over $107 billion in expenditures involving more than 4 million suppliers. Clients such as Hewlett-Packard (HP), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Anthropic benefitted from Zip's services, enabling them to enhance efficiency and achieve tangible outcomes.

news.headline: TeleSafety reveals new workplace safety solution at Dirt World, aiming to reduce accidents and lower comp costs.
news.summary: Top civil construction and infrastructure development companies were recently introduced to innovative technology aimed at minimizing accidents and lowering workers' compensation expenses in Orchard Park, New York. On December 10th, 2024, TeleSafety, a trailblazer in workplace safety technology, unveiled their groundbreaking solution. This technology has the potential to significantly decrease workplace incidents and cut costs related to workers' compensation.

news.headline: Parallax names Jeff Eckerle as new CEO to guide company's growth and innovation, succeeding Tom O'Neill.
news.summary: In Edina, Minnesota, Parallax, a prominent provider of predictive forecasting and resource management software, revealed the appointment of Jeff Eckerle as its new CEO. Eckerle takes over from Tom O’Neill, the founding CEO, who spearheaded the initial growth of the company, positioning it as an innovator in assisting digital service entities in optimizing their team productivity. The platform developed by Parallax caters to professional services firms, offering advanced predictive features.

news.headline: Transportation Insight launches Margin Management in Beon Insight to track customer profitability efficiently.
news.summary: In Atlanta on December 10, 2024, Transportation Insight (TI) unveiled Margin Management as part of Beon Insight, its sophisticated data and analytics platform designed for various business requirements. This new feature utilizes data inputs and artificial intelligence to track and enhance profitability.

news.headline: HopSkipDrive expands to Atlanta after regulatory changes, bringing safe school transportation solutions to Georgia.
news.summary: HopSkipDrive, a tech firm addressing school transportation issues focusing on safety, equity, and care, is expanding to Atlanta. The State Board of Education in Georgia approved regulatory changes permitting school districts to employ small vehicle transport, benefiting Atlanta schools. This move provides schools with secure, dependable, and fair mobility solutions, supporting the company's mission.

news.headline: CFP Board set record with over 10,000 test-takers in Nov 2024; 3,755 candidates took exam, 6% passed.
news.summary: Over 10,000 individuals participated in the CFP® Exam in 2024, as reported by CFP Board. The exam took place from November 6 to 13, with a total of 3,755 candidates taking the test. Results showed that 6% of the candidates who sat for the exam passed.

news.headline: Dolfin completes $23 million TI lease for Applied Underwriters at Rockefeller Center, NYC.
news.summary: In New York on December 10, 2024, Dolfin, a groundbreaking financial technology company specializing in tenant improvement financing solutions, revealed the achievement of a $23 million tenant improvement (TI) lease for Applied Underwriters, Inc. This lease was a crucial step in supporting the operations of Applied...

news.headline: LG Electronics and University of Alaska Anchorage launch heat pump labs in Alaska for cold-climate HVAC research.
news.summary: A pioneering partnership between LG Electronics Inc. and the Consortium for Advanced Heat Pump Research has launched real-world laboratories in Anchorage and Fairbanks, Alaska. The initiative, announced on December 10, 2024, aims to enhance heat pump performance in tundra conditions. This collaborative effort seeks to optimize the efficiency and effectiveness of heat pumps, in a region where extreme cold weather presents unique challenges. LG's involvement in this consortium underscores its commitment to innovation and sustainability in the heating technology sector.

news.headline: InEight aims for FedRAMP approval to offer cloud construction document control solution to federal agencies.
news.summary: InEight has recently announced its initiation of the Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) certification process for its InEight Document software in Scottsdale, Arizona. This certification process involves rigorous security assessments and compliance checks to ensure the software meets the strict security standards set by the government. FedRAMP certification is crucial for software vendors looking to provide their services to federal agencies, as it demonstrates a high level of data protection and risk management. The certification process typically involves extensive documentation, testing, and evaluation, with an average timeline ranging from 6 to 18 months. For InEight, achieving FedRAMP certification for its Document software could open up new opportunities in the government sector and solidify its position as a trusted solution provider for federal projects.

news.headline: Marelli's Passenger Display with Privacy wins 'Challenger' at Digital Engineering Awards 2024 in Detroit.
news.summary: Marelli's Passenger Display with Privacy has been awarded the prestigious 'Challenger' honor at The Digital Engineering Awards 2024 held in Detroit on December 10, 2024. The innovative technology secured this recognition in the "Engineering Product of the Year" category. Marelli's cutting-edge display offers enhanced privacy features, revolutionizing the passenger experience. The award ceremony celebrated technological advancements, with Marelli's product standing out among competitors. This accolade solidifies Marelli's position as a leader in engineering and technology, showcasing their commitment to innovation in the industry.

news.headline: Rory Olson named MacDonald-Miller's CEO, established contractor with over 100 years of experience in the industry.
news.summary: A recent report reveals that the global poverty rate has dropped significantly over the past two decades. The number of people living in extreme poverty (defined as living on less than $1.90 per day) has decreased from 1.9 billion in 1990 to 689 million in 2017. This progress has been particularly marked in East Asia and the Pacific region, where extreme poverty has fallen from 60% in 1990 to just 3.5% in 2017. However, Sub-Saharan Africa still remains the region with the highest poverty rates, with 27.2% of the population living in extreme poverty in 2017. The report emphasizes the importance of continuing efforts to eradicate poverty globally.

news.headline: TransThera announces Phase I results of TT-01488, a non-covalent BTK inhibitor, for B-cell malignancies. Based in Nanjing and Gaithersburg.
news.summary: On December 10, 2024, in Nanjing, China and Gaithersburg, Maryland, TransThera Sciences announced its latest developments. TransThera, a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, is dedicated to discovering and developing new small molecule therapies. This company focuses on meeting clinical demands and has reached the registrational clinical stage. TransThera is committed to bringing innovative treatments to market.
